text: Can a sech^2 soliton IC for v in the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system (with u perturbed from the m=0 Gardner reduction by 0.2*v) survive to T=8.0 as a single dominant peak with amplitude >= 1.0 and mass drift < 8%?

method: Fourier pseudospectral IMEX-CN: CN on v_xxx (dispersive for v), explicit on nonlinear; for u: fully explicit forward Euler; 2/3 dealiasing; dt=5e-4
bank_use_rationale: Negative entries: avoid explicit-only for v_xxx (fragmentation), avoid no-dealiasing (aliasing artifacts), avoid central FD for Burgers, keep dt small enough for amplitude-CFL. Used 2/3 dealiasing and IMEX-CN with dt=5e-4.

diagnostics.overflow_in: v*v_x and u*v nonlinear terms within first steps

rationale: Blow-up at dt=5e-4 despite 2/3 dealiasing and IMEX-CN on v_xxx. Root cause: the u equation uses forward Euler and receives stiff forcing -d/dx(v_xx) = -v_xxx which in Fourier is ik^3 * v_hat — exactly the same dispersive stiffness operator as v's own v_xxx term. This was not stabilized. Fix: treat this cross-coupling stiff term semi-implicitly (CN-average over old and new v states).

Use operator-split IMEX: (1) advance v with CN on v_xxx; (2) advance u using explicit nonlinear plus CN-averaged Lv*v forcing (ik^3*v), using the new v_hat already computed. Also reduce dt to 2e-4 for extra safety margin.

method: Fourier pseudospectral operator-split IMEX-CN: (1) advance v with CN on ik^3 dispersive, explicit nonlinear; (2) advance u with explicit -3u*u_x - d/dx(3v^2) plus semi-implicit CN-averaged ik^3*v forcing for -d/dx(v_xx) term; 2/3 dealiasing; dt=2e-4
bank_use_rationale: From F1 failure: confirmed that explicit u-step blows up due to ik^3*v stiff coupling (diagnosed in-session). Negative bank kb-gardner-GardnerIsM0-coupledSystemInstability confirms full coupled system can be harder than isolated Gardner. Now using semi-implicit treatment of ik^3*v forcing in u's equation (CN-average), dt reduced to 2e-4 for additional safety.

rationale: Partial progress: solution stable to t~2 then blows up. Multiple v-peaks already forming by t=1 (4 peaks) indicating aliasing or instability from the coupling. u grows to 15 at t=2 driven by the soliton forcing. Root cause: the coupling term -d/dx(u*v) in v's equation amplifies as u grows, creating spurious v peaks; the u equation has no stiffness control for its own nonlinear advection 3u*u_x as u grows large. Need adaptive timestepping that can automatically handle the growing u field.

Switch to scipy RK45 with adaptive dt (max_step=1e-3). This handles all stiffness automatically. Keep 2/3 dealiasing on all nonlinear terms. The adaptive stepper will reduce dt near the high-energy events (t~2-4) and avoid blow-up.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + scipy RK45 adaptive integrator, rtol=1e-4, atol=1e-6, max_step=1e-3; 2/3 dealiasing on all nonlinear products; spectral derivatives for all terms including v_xxx
bank_use_rationale: All negative entries inform avoidance of explicit-only, non-dealiased, or Burgers central-FD approaches. RK45 adaptive stepping directly addresses the amplitude-dependent CFL failure pattern documented in kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL-amplitude-boundary by letting the solver automatically reduce dt when the nonlinear speed increases.

rationale: Full integration to T=8 succeeded with no NaN. Single dominant peak (0.63) at T=8 — 2.71x larger than second peak. Mass perfectly conserved (0% drift). Fields bounded (max|u|=6.03 < 15). However, dominant peak amplitude 0.63 < 1.0 threshold. Cross-verification with tight tolerances (rtol=1e-7) confirms this is physical decay, not numerical artifact. The soliton decays because (1) KdV sech^2 is not an exact soliton of the coupled system (as per kb-gardner-sech2IC-not-exact-soliton), (2) the Gardner reduction m=0 is not conserved by the coupled system dynamics, and (3) u grows to ~6 and drives energy out of v via the coupling term.

3-iteration limit reached. E3 (RK45 adaptive) gives the physically correct and numerically stable result: stable soliton structure survived (single dominant peak, 2.71x above second), mass conserved, bounded. The 0.63 amplitude is below the 1.0 criterion but is the physically correct behavior of this IC in this system.
